title: Listening - Part 1
description: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best.
instructions: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear. There are two questions for each extract.

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing a new language-learning app they are using.
extracts.audio_script: Man: How are you finding 'Lingo-Leap'? I’ve finally mastered the past tense in Italian. || Woman: [1]I’m finding the speech recognition software a bit hit-and-miss[/1]. It keeps telling me my pronunciation is wrong even when I’m mimicking the audio exactly. || Man: I had the same issue! I think it struggles with background noise. || Woman: [2]What I do like, though, is the focus on practical, everyday phrases[/2]. I feel like I could actually order a coffee or ask for directions now. || Man: I agree. It’s much better than those old courses that started with 'the apple is on the table'. || Woman: Exactly. It’s a bit more relevant to real life, which keeps me motivated to practice every morning.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: How are you finding 'Lingo-Leap'? I’ve finally mastered the past tense in Italian.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I’m finding the speech recognition software a bit hit-and-miss. It keeps telling me my pronunciation is wrong even when I’m mimicking the audio exactly.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I had the same issue! I think it struggles with background noise.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: What I do like, though, is the focus on practical, everyday phrases. I feel like I could actually order a coffee or ask for directions now.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It’s much better than those old courses that started with 'the apple is on the table'.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. It’s a bit more relevant to real life, which keeps me motivated to practice every morning.

questions.question: The woman is frustrated with the app because

options.A: the grammar explanations are too complex.
options.B: the voice analysis tool is unreliable.
options.C: the audio quality of the native speakers is poor.

questions.explanation: She describes the speech recognition as 'hit-and-miss' and says it corrects her incorrectly [1].

questions.question: Both speakers agree that the app's strength lies in its
questions.explanation: They both praise the focus on 'practical, everyday phrases' and 'real life' relevance [2].

extracts.context: You hear two colleagues talking about a professional conference held online.
extracts.audio_script: Man: That virtual summit was surprisingly well-organized. I didn't experience a single technical glitch. || Woman: [3]I suspect the organizers were playing it safe by pre-recording all the main keynote speeches[/3], which took away the excitement of a live event. || Man: That would explain why there were no delays. But I missed the live interaction. || Woman: [4]I’ve noticed that the 'breakout rooms' for networking were far too large to be effective[/4]. You can't have a meaningful conversation with fifty people at once. || Man: I agree. It just became a shouting match or complete silence. || Woman: Exactly. If they do it again next year, they really need to cap those groups at five or six people.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That virtual summit was surprisingly well-organized. I didn't experience a single technical glitch.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I suspect the organizers were playing it safe by pre-recording all the main keynote speeches, which took away the excitement of a live event.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That would explain why there were no delays. But I missed the live interaction.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I’ve noticed that the 'breakout rooms' for networking were far too large to be effective. You can't have a meaningful conversation with fifty people at once.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It just became a shouting match or complete silence.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. If they do it again next year, they really need to cap those groups at five or six people.

questions.question: The woman believes the conference lacked spontaneity because

options.B: the sessions were not broadcast in real-time.
options.C: the audience was not allowed to ask questions.

questions.explanation: She mentions the speeches were 'pre-recorded', which took away the 'excitement of a live event' [3].

questions.question: What is the speakers' shared opinion on the networking sessions?
questions.explanation: They both agree that having fifty people in a 'breakout room' was 'far too large to be effective' [4].

extracts.context: You hear two people discussing a new brand of eco-friendly household cleaners.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I’ve been using those refillable cleaning sprays from 'Pure-Home'. They actually smell quite nice for a change. || Woman: [5]I found the initial cost of the glass bottles to be a bit steep[/5], even if the refill sachets are cheap later on. || Man: It’s an investment, I suppose. But they do look better on the counter than plastic ones. || Woman: [6]I did appreciate that the products actually work on tough stains[/6]. Usually, 'natural' cleaners are a bit useless against grease, but these were surprisingly effective. || Man: I agree. I was skeptical, but the kitchen degreaser is a game-changer. || Woman: It’s good to find a brand that doesn't compromise on performance just to be environmentally friendly.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I’ve been using those refillable cleaning sprays from 'Pure-Home'. They actually smell quite nice for a change.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I found the initial cost of the glass bottles to be a bit steep, even if the refill sachets are cheap later on.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: It’s an investment, I suppose. But they do look better on the counter than plastic ones.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I did appreciate that the products actually work on tough stains. Usually, 'natural' cleaners are a bit useless against grease, but these were surprisingly effective.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. I was skeptical, but the kitchen degreaser is a game-changer.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It’s good to find a brand that doesn't compromise on performance just to be environmentally friendly.

questions.question: What was the woman's main reservation about the cleaning products?

options.A: the difficulty of the refill process
options.B: the high price of the starting kit

questions.explanation: She found the 'initial cost of the glass bottles to be a bit steep' [5].

questions.question: Both speakers were impressed by the brand's
questions.explanation: They both highlight that the products 'actually work on tough stains' and are 'surprisingly effective' [6].
